description: Stage 1 found biological signal and a leakage source, but no cross-batch train anchors or valid substitute for held-out perturbation/dose teacher targets, so architecture search was not reopened.

User corrected that CRISPR-style perturbation does not require chemical dose. Genetic-anchor synthetic audit passes reopening via train cross-batch anchors plus held-out eval cross-batch replicates. Norman h5ad has no batch metadata and dose_val is guide-count notation, so batch/dose are ignored for Norman-specific experiment.

Minimal BioTech-JEPA ran on paired synthetic genetic-anchor data. It is a real JEPA path with EMA targets and cross-modal/action-conditioned latent prediction, but single-seed low-compute signal is insufficient for promotion.
Norman processed h5ad ran with batch and chemical dose ignored per user instruction. This is an RNA-only diagnostic and cannot validate cross-modal imaging or batch disentanglement.
